gloss.en: the leader of a republic or organization

words.example: Several presidents attended the funeral.

gloss.en: newspapers and magazines

words.example: The story was reported in the national press.

gloss.en: force used to persuade or push someone

words.example: The pressure for change continued to grow.

gloss.en: happening or existing before something else

words.example: No previous experience is necessary for this job.

gloss.en: a person who performs religious ceremonies

words.example: The local priest visited the family.

gloss.en: very simple or from an early stage

words.example: They lived in very primitive conditions.

gloss.en: a son or male relative of a king

words.example: The young prince waved to the crowd.

gloss.en: a daughter or female relative of a king

words.example: The princess smiled at the children.

gloss.en: making letters or pictures on paper

words.example: The invention of printing changed the world.

gloss.en: a building where criminals are kept

words.example: She went to prison for tax evasion.

gloss.en: a person kept in prison

words.example: The prisoner was released early.

gloss.en: for one person or group, not public

words.example: The sign said, 'Private property. Keep out.'

gloss.en: an award for winning or doing good work

words.example: She was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

gloss.en: delaying things you should do now

words.example: Procrastination made him miss the deadline.

gloss.en: a person or company that makes something

gloss.en: the process of making goods or food

words.example: Oil production has increased this year.

gloss.en: a job needing special training or skill

words.example: She joined the teaching profession.

gloss.en: a university teacher of the highest rank

words.example: The professor gave an excellent lecture.

gloss.en: coded instructions that control a computer

words.example: You need to install the program on your computer.

gloss.en: an official order stopping something

words.example: There is a prohibition on smoking here.

gloss.en: to help sell or make something popular

words.example: The band toured to promote their new album.

gloss.en: a word used in place of a noun

words.example: 'He' and 'she' are each a pronoun.

gloss.en: right, correct or suitable

words.example: We should have had a proper discussion first.

gloss.en: in a correct or suitable way

words.example: How much money do we need to do the job properly?

gloss.en: a thing or things that someone owns

words.example: The police returned the stolen property.

gloss.en: rich and successful

words.example: The town grew into a prosperous city.

gloss.en: to keep someone or something safe

words.example: We must protect children from harm.

gloss.en: a statement or action against something

words.example: They organized a protest against the new law.

gloss.en: pleased about something you did or own

words.example: They are proud parents.

gloss.en: to show that something is true with facts

words.example: This new evidence will prove her innocence.

gloss.en: a short well-known saying with advice

words.example: There is an old proverb about hard work.

gloss.en: only if something happens

words.example: You can go provided you finish your work.

gloss.en: a place where people drink and meet

words.example: We're all going to the pub after work.

gloss.en: connected with ordinary people in general

words.example: The campaign aims to raise public awareness.

gloss.en: in a way seen or known by everyone

words.example: He publicly apologized for his mistake.

gloss.en: a person or company that produces books

words.example: The publisher accepted her first novel.

gloss.en: to move something toward you with force

words.example: Please pull the door to open it.

gloss.en: a knitted sweater pulled over the head

words.example: She wore a warm wool pullover.

gloss.en: to make someone suffer for doing wrong

words.example: Those responsible for the crime will be punished.

gloss.en: a way of making someone suffer for wrong

words.example: The punishment seemed too harsh.

gloss.en: a young dog

words.example: The children played with the new puppy.

gloss.en: to make something clean or pure

words.example: The filter helps purify the water.

gloss.en: a small bag for money

words.example: She took some coins from her purse.

gloss.en: to move something away with force

words.example: He gave the door a gentle push.

gloss.en: a sudden shaking of the ground

words.example: The quake destroyed several old buildings.

gloss.en: having the training to do a job

words.example: She is a qualified teacher.

gloss.en: to gain the skill or right to do something

words.example: How long does it take to qualify?

gloss.en: how good or bad something is

words.example: We want to improve the quality of care.

gloss.en: an amount or number of something

words.example: They bought a large quantity of food.

gloss.en: a line of people waiting for something

words.example: There was a long queue at the bank.

gloss.en: a competition to see who is fastest

words.example: He won the horse race.

gloss.en: the sport of competing for speed

words.example: He likes to watch horse racing on TV.

gloss.en: a track on which trains run

words.example: They built a new railroad across the desert.

gloss.en: a track with rails for trains

words.example: The railway is still under construction.

gloss.en: the amount of rain that falls

words.example: The region has heavy rainfall in summer.

gloss.en: a thick forest in a hot rainy area

words.example: Many animals live in the rainforest.

gloss.en: not happening or seen very often

words.example: It is a very rare disease.

gloss.en: not very often

words.example: She is rarely seen in public nowadays.

gloss.en: a small soft red fruit

words.example: She picked a fresh raspberry from the bush.

gloss.en: a fixed amount of food allowed

words.example: Each soldier received a daily ration of food.

gloss.en: a sharp tool for shaving hair

words.example: He shaved with a new razor.

gloss.en: to behave or change in response to something

words.example: I nudged her but she didn't react.

gloss.en: the true situation that actually exists

words.example: She refuses to face reality.

gloss.en: to organize things in a different way

words.example: We had to rearrange the meeting for Friday.

gloss.en: a paper showing something was paid for

words.example: Can I have a receipt, please?

gloss.en: the entrance desk in a hotel or office

words.example: Please report to reception on arrival.

gloss.en: to say something aloud from memory

words.example: The child can recite the whole poem.

gloss.en: to get something back

words.example: You can reclaim your lost luggage here.

gloss.en: to know someone or something seen before

words.example: I didn't recognise you with your new haircut.

gloss.en: to know someone or something seen before

words.example: I recognized him as soon as he came in.

gloss.en: to say something is good or suitable

words.example: Can you recommend a good restaurant?

gloss.en: a written account kept for the future

words.example: You should keep a record of your expenses.

gloss.en: sound or pictures saved to play later

words.example: They made an audio recording of the talk.

gloss.en: made from used materials again

words.example: The bags are made from recycled paper.

gloss.en: treating used things to use them again

words.example: The city has a good recycling program.

gloss.en: to make something smaller or less

words.example: We need to reduce our spending this month.

gloss.en: a ridge of rock or coral near the sea surface

words.example: Divers explored the colourful coral reef.

gloss.en: to show an image on a surface

words.example: His face was reflected in the mirror.

gloss.en: light food and drinks

words.example: Refreshments will be served after the meeting.

gloss.en: money paid back to you

words.example: The shop gave me a full refund.

gloss.en: to say you will not do something

words.example: He may refuse to help us.

gloss.en: about or concerning something

words.example: I have a question regarding your report.

gloss.en: a large area of land

words.example: They live in a mountainous region.

gloss.en: at regular times or intervals

words.example: We meet regularly to discuss the project.

gloss.en: to refuse to accept something

words.example: They decided to reject the plan.

gloss.en: to show a connection between things

words.example: I found it hard to relate the two ideas.

gloss.en: the way people or groups deal with each other

gloss.en: the way people or groups connect

words.example: They have a close working relationship.

gloss.en: judged by comparing with something else

words.example: Consider the relative merits of the two plans.

gloss.en: calm and not worried

words.example: He appeared relaxed and confident.

gloss.en: helping you rest and feel calm

gloss.en: the act of setting someone free

gloss.en: able to be trusted to do well

gloss.en: to continue to be in the same state

gloss.en: the act of remembering someone or something

gloss.en: to help someone remember something

words.example: Can you remind me of your name?

gloss.en: far away from other places or people

gloss.en: to take something away from a place

gloss.en: the act of fixing something

gloss.en: to pay money back to someone

words.example: He promised to repay the loan next year.

gloss.en: to say or do something again

gloss.en: happening or done many times

gloss.en: many times; again and again

gloss.en: to act or speak for a group

gloss.en: the act of asking for something politely

gloss.en: to need something

gloss.en: a person who studies to find new facts

gloss.en: a seat or room kept for you

gloss.en: a supply something useful that can be used

gloss.en: strong approval of someone's qualities

gloss.en: considered good and proper by people

gloss.en: to give an answer or reaction

gloss.en: a duty to take care of something

gloss.en: having a duty to care for something
